source_jobs.title: Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Rune

Rune Technologies builds mission-critical software for military logistics and sustainment. Our flagship product is TyrOS, an AI-enabled logistics command and control platform that gives commanders a single, real-time operating picture of their sustainment fight, from inventory and distribution to medical and operational planning, even in degraded and contested environments.



We're not a traditional defense contractor. We're a small, fast-moving team that combines deep operational experience in and with the DoD with the software discipline of the best product companies, and we apply that combination to a customer set that desperately needs it. We stay close to our users by embedding directly with military units during exercises and rotations, and we ship constantly based on what we learn.



The U.S. military and its allies face a real logistics modernization problem. We're building the software to solve it.




ABOUT THE ROLE

A logistics officer planning a resupply across contested terrain has minutes to make decisions that move thousands of tons of equipment and shape whether an operation succeeds. The software they use to make those calls is the difference between the right plan and the wrong one. That’s the product problem at Rune.



The capabilities we choose to build – and how we scope, sequence, and ship them – determine how commanders see their fight, plan their sustainment, and hold their operating picture together when conditions degrade. Product strategy is how we win.



We’re looking for a Product Manager with strong technical instincts, sharp product judgment, and the agency to own significant pieces of TyrOS end-to-end. You’ll partner with engineering, design, mission managers, and growth to define our roadmap, scope and ship our most complex capabilities, and stay close to users in the field. You’ll have more direct access to real users in real conditions than almost anywhere else you could work.



This is our second product hire. You’ll have strong influence on what we build, how we build it, and how the product function takes shape as we grow.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own product strategy and roadmap for major areas of TyrOS, from problem framing through shipped capability and iteration

 - Define and ship our most complex features working hand-in-hand with engineering on architecture, tradeoffs, and what’s actually buildable

 - Embed with military units during exercises and rotations to learn the domain firsthand, build trust with users, and translate field reality into product direction

 - Partner tightly with design to turn complex problems into intuitive product experiences that hold up under pressure

 - Work closely with our growth and mission success team to understand customer and contract context, and translate it into prioritized product work

 - Bring a strong point of view to cross-functional discussions: advocate for the right bets, push back on the wrong ones, and make tradeoffs explicit




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3+ years as a product manager and 6-10+ years of total professional experience, with a track record of owning meaningful product surfaces end-to-end

 - Technical credibility – a CS background, prior engineering experience, or equivalent depth that lets you go deep with engineers on architecture, capabilities, and tradeoffs. You don’t need to be writing code, but you need to understand how software gets built

 - Experience at strong product companies – big tech, top-tier startups, or other organizations known for a high product bar – where you’ve seen what good looks like

 - Strong product judgment: you can frame ambiguous problems, scope smartly, and make sharp tradeoffs under uncertainty

 - Demonstrated ability to ship intuitive product experiences in close partnership with great designers

 - Excellent written and verbal communication, with the ability to tailor your message to engineers, designers, customers, and executives

 - Willingness and desire to get into the

source_jobs.title: Mission Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Rune

Rune Technologies builds mission-critical software for military logistics and sustainment. Our flagship product is TyrOS, an AI-enabled logistics command and control platform that gives commanders a single, real-time operating picture of their sustainment fight, from inventory and distribution to medical and operational planning, even in degraded and contested environments.



We're not a traditional defense contractor. We're a small, fast-moving team that combines deep operational experience in and with the DoD with the software discipline of the best product companies, and we apply that combination to a customer set that desperately needs it. We stay close to our users by embedding directly with military units during exercises and rotations, and we ship constantly based on what we learn.



The U.S. military and its allies face a real logistics modernization problem. We're building the software to solve it.





About the Role
Our mission managers are the linchpin connecting our innovative engineering and product teams with our military partners as we execute contracted work. If you excel at building deep, trust-based relationships, translating complex operational needs into actionable product and program strategies, and thriving in high-ownership customer-facing roles, then you’re the ideal candidate for our mission manager team. In this role, you’ll ensure our solutions meet the rigorous demands of modern military logistics – driving success from the remote tactical edge to the highest levels of command.



What You’ll Do

 - Work side-by-side with our customers to understand their unique challenges and represent the customer as you work with the Rune engineering team to shape product development.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with Rune engineering, product, business development and executive teams to ensure each team is engaged and working towards customer success.

 - Be comfortable that no two weeks in the life of a Mission Manager will be the same; be a do-er who wants to understand customer problems and highly technical solutions to those problems.

 - Support deployment of Rune software for testing, evaluation and operational use at government facilities and military bases.

 - Lead training sessions to ensure Rune’s product meets user needs, and to ensure it is being used to have a concrete impact on our customers’ operations.

 - Identify relevant datasets through deep engagement with customer problems and workflows, and work with Rune engineers to integrate this data into product delivery.



Qualifications

 - Minimum of 4 years of experience serving in the US military in logistics or sustainment roles preferred

 - Excellent organizational, leadership, and decision-making skills

 - Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills to interact with customers, team members, and senior leadership

 - Ability to work effectively with cross-disciplinary teams, including engineering, product, operations and growth

 - Willingness to travel 25-40% to support program objectives and customer engagements

 - Bachelor’s Degree required

 - Must be eligible to obtain and maintain a U.S. secret security clearance
   
   

Benefits

Rune offers top-tier benefits for full-time employees to include a full suite of insurance options at no cost for employees and low-cost to spouses and dependents. Highly competitive equity grants are also  included in the majority of full time offers and are considered part of Rune's total compensation package. Benefits include:

 - Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans; premiums 100% covered by Rune for all employees; exceptionally low premiums for spouses and dependents

 - Basic life insurance and disability 100% covered for all employees by Rune; option to purchase additional life insurance available

 - ‘Take the time off that you need, when you need it’ paid time off, not accrual based

 - Generous company holiday calendar including a holiday shutdown in Decembe

source_jobs.title: Backend Software Engineer - Sensors and Tracking
source_jobs.description:
About Rune

Rune Technologies builds mission-critical software for military logistics and sustainment. Our flagship product is TyrOS, an AI-enabled logistics command and control platform that gives commanders a single, real-time operating picture of their sustainment fight, from inventory and distribution to medical and operational planning, even in degraded and contested environments.



We're not a traditional defense contractor. We're a small, fast-moving team that combines deep operational experience in and with the DoD with the software discipline of the best product companies, and we apply that combination to a customer set that desperately needs it. We stay close to our users by embedding directly with military units during exercises and rotations, and we ship constantly based on what we learn.



The U.S. military and its allies face a real logistics modernization problem. We're building the software to solve it.




ABOUT THE ROLE

At Rune Technologies, we are building the logistics technology backbone that the U.S. and allied militaries need to dominate in large-scale combat operations. As a Backend Software Engineer at Rune, you will be part of a high-impact team working at the intersection of advanced logistics software, physical systems, and complex data integrations—all in support of the warfighter at the tactical edge.

You'll join us in our mission to bring 21st-century software to the sustainment domain: modernizing inventory management, transportation optimization, and operational planning with state-of-the-art user experiences. You'll work closely with a team of experienced engineers, product leaders, and operational experts who care deeply about delivering real-world impact.

As our Backend Software Engineer for Sensors and Tracking, you will be the first engineer dedicated to this workstream within TyrOS. You will build the systems that ingest data directly from physical hardware and sensors—including fuel-level sensors, munitions-count systems, and pallet-scanning technologies—and transform that data into reliable, usable information within the platform. You will also help develop tracking and sensor-fusion capabilities that correlate detections from multiple sources into a coherent view of a single entity or object.

This is a ground-floor role with significant ownership. You will not be inheriting an established team or mature playbook, so we are looking for an experienced software engineer who is comfortable operating in ambiguity, solving difficult hardware-to-software integration problems, and building production systems from the ground up.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build and own production-grade backend systems that ingest data directly from physical sensors, devices, and hardware platforms.

 - Develop integrations for a range of sensor and hardware systems, working with clean APIs where available and finding reliable ways to extract data where interfaces are incomplete, poorly documented, or nonexistent.

 - Design services and data pipelines that normalize, process, and make sensor data available within TyrOS.

 - Build and improve tracking and sensor-fusion capabilities that correlate detections from multiple sources into a single tracked entity.

 - Work with imperfect real-world data, including incomplete, delayed, inconsistent, or unreliable feeds.

 - Collaborate with platform engineers, product teams, and operational stakeholders to translate sensor and tracking requirements into dependable software.

 - Deploy and test software in both unclassified and classified environments, including periodic work at customer or government sites.

 - Participate in technical design discussions and code reviews, contributing to a culture of engineering rigor, ownership, and mission focus.

 - Triage and debug production issues, improving system reliability, observability, and performance through rapid iteration.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 4+ years of experience in software engineering, with demonstrated experience

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Rune

Rune Technologies builds mission-critical software for military logistics and sustainment. Our flagship product is TyrOS, an AI-enabled logistics command and control platform that gives commanders a single, real-time operating picture of their sustainment fight, from inventory and distribution to medical and operational planning, even in degraded and contested environments.



We're not a traditional defense contractor. We're a small, fast-moving team that combines deep operational experience in and with the DoD with the software discipline of the best product companies, and we apply that combination to a customer set that desperately needs it. We stay close to our users by embedding directly with military units during exercises and rotations, and we ship constantly based on what we learn.



The U.S. military and its allies face a real logistics modernization problem. We're building the software to solve it.





About the Role

At Rune Technologies, we are building the logistics technology backbone that the U.S. and allied militaries need to dominate in large-scale combat operations. As a Full Stack Software Engineer at Rune, you will be part of a high-impact, customer-driven team working at the intersection of advanced logistics algorithms, modern AI enabled software, and complex system integrations — all in support of the warfighter at the tactical edge.

You’ll join us in our mission to bring 21st-century software to the sustainment domain — modernizing inventory management, transportation optimization, and predictive maintenance with state-of-the-art user experiences. You’ll work closely with a team of experienced engineers, product designers, and operational experts who care deeply about delivering real-world impact to complex domain problems in the sustainment enterprise.



What You’ll Do

 - Design, build, and deploy robust software solutions that address the DoD’s hardest logistics technology challenges.

 - Understand, break down, model and integrate complex real world sustainment doctrine and tactics, techniques and procedures (TTPs) into Rune’s data models and technology solutions.

 - Build and iterate on applications that deliver modern, intuitive experiences to users at the tactical edge.

 - Integrate logistics data from diverse systems to enable real-time visibility, predictive insights, and optimized workflows.

 - Implement challenging algorithmic backend solutions in a robust, scalable way to solve complex real-world logistics challenges faced by Rune’s customers.

 - Participate in technical design discussions and code reviews, contributing to a culture of excellence, collaboration, and mission focus.

 - Triage and debug issues in production, ensuring high reliability and rapid iteration in support of operational needs.

 - Work closely with military stakeholders, engineers, and product managers to ensure our technology meets real-world sustainment requirements.



Qualifications

 - 4+ years of experience in software engineering, preferably building and deploying production-grade web applications or distributed systems.

 - Strong coding skills with demonstrated proficiency in programming languages such as Go, Python, TypeScript, or similar.

 - Experience working with data pipelines, APIs, and system integrations.

 - Strong sense of ownership and a bias for action — you thrive in fast-paced, dynamic environments where your work directly impacts mission outcomes.

 - Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to translate user needs into technical solutions.



Desired Qualifications

 - Experience working on software in defense, aerospace, or other complex operational domains.

 - Familiarity with logistics, supply chain, or sustainment challenges — especially in military contexts.

 - Experience deploying software to production environments in managed cloud infrastructure (e.g. AWS, Kubernetes) as well as Edge infrastructure (small form factor hardware).

 - Expos

source_jobs.title: Talent Programs Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
About Rune

Rune Technologies builds mission-critical software for military logistics and sustainment. Our flagship product is TyrOS, an AI-enabled logistics command and control platform that gives commanders a single, real-time operating picture of their sustainment fight, from inventory and distribution to medical and operational planning, even in degraded and contested environments.



We're not a traditional defense contractor. We're a small, fast-moving team that combines deep operational experience in and with the DoD with the software discipline of the best product companies, and we apply that combination to a customer set that desperately needs it. We stay close to our users by embedding directly with military units during exercises and rotations, and we ship constantly based on what we learn.



The U.S. military and its allies face a real logistics modernization problem. We're building the software to solve it.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Talent Programs Coordinator at Rune, you will play a critical role in helping us attract, engage, and hire the exceptional people needed to accomplish that mission. Working at the intersection of recruiting, operations, and executive support, you will help create a world-class candidate experience while enabling our leadership team to operate at maximum effectiveness.

You'll join us in our mission to bring 21st-century software to the sustainment domain—modernizing inventory management, transportation optimization, and predictive maintenance with state-of-the-art technology. As one of the first touchpoints for many candidates, you'll help shape how top talent experiences Rune while building the operational systems that allow our team to scale quickly and efficiently. You'll work closely with recruiters, hiring managers, company leadership, and our co-founders to ensure that hiring and business operations run seamlessly.

As a Talent Programs Coordinator, you will own the coordination and execution of critical recruiting and leadership operations that keep Rune moving forward. From managing complex interview schedules and delivering a high-touch candidate experience to supporting executive calendars and company-wide initiatives, you'll serve as a trusted operational partner across the organization. We're looking for someone who thrives in fast-paced environments, loves bringing order to complexity, and takes pride in creating exceptional experiences for candidates, teammates, and leaders alike.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Interview Coordination & Candidate Experience

 - Schedule phone, virtual, and onsite interviews across multiple teams and time zones.

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for candidates throughout the interview process.

 - Ensure candidates receive timely communication, interview details, and preparation materials.

 - Coordinate travel arrangements and onsite logistics when required.

 - Deliver a high-touch, professional, and welcoming candidate experience.

Recruiting Operations

 - Maintain accurate candidate records and workflows within the Applicant Tracking System (ATS).

 - Track interview feedback and ensure timely completion by interviewers.

 - Support offer process logistics and onboarding coordination.

 - Monitor recruiting metrics and help maintain recruiting dashboards and reports.

 - Assist with job posting management across recruiting platforms.

 - Support recruiting events, university recruiting initiatives, and networking activities.

 - Identify opportunities to improve recruiting processes and operational efficiency.

Founder & Executive Support

 - Support Rune's co-founders with calendar management, meeting coordination, and scheduling priorities.

 - Prepare meeting materials, agendas, and briefing documents to ensure leaders are well-prepared for internal and external meetings.

 - Coordinate logistics for customer meetings, recruiting activities, leadership offsites, and company events.

 - Track action items and follow-ups from key meeting

source_jobs.title: Cloud Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Rune

Rune Technologies builds mission-critical software for military logistics and sustainment. Our flagship product is TyrOS, an AI-enabled logistics command and control platform that gives commanders a single, real-time operating picture of their sustainment fight, from inventory and distribution to medical and operational planning, even in degraded and contested environments.



We're not a traditional defense contractor. We're a small, fast-moving team that combines deep operational experience in and with the DoD with the software discipline of the best product companies, and we apply that combination to a customer set that desperately needs it. We stay close to our users by embedding directly with military units during exercises and rotations, and we ship constantly based on what we learn.



The U.S. military and its allies face a real logistics modernization problem. We're building the software to solve it.



About the Role

At Rune, we are building the infrastructure that powers logistics operations across cloud and edge environments — from centralized systems to distributed nodes operating in constrained and disconnected conditions. As a Cloud DevOps Engineer, you will be responsible for scaling and operating the cloud backbone that supports this system.

This is not a traditional cloud role. You will take a functional, early-stage platform and scale it into a highly available, multi-environment system, capable of coordinating with edge deployments and supporting distributed workloads. You’ll work on infrastructure that must remain reliable while interacting with thousands of nodes across heterogeneous environments, including systems with intermittent connectivity.

You will design and operate cloud infrastructure across AWS and multi-cloud environments, build and maintain Kubernetes-based deployment systems, and ensure that our platform can scale, recover, and evolve as usage grows. You’ll also help bridge the gap between cloud and edge — enabling synchronization, deployment, and observability across a distributed mesh of systems.

This role combines platform engineering, deployment engineering, and SRE, with a strong emphasis on building systems that actually run in production — not just in ideal cloud conditions.



What You’ll Do

 - Design, build, and operate cloud infrastructure across AWS and multi-cloud environments.

 - Deploy and manage applications on Kubernetes (EKS and other environments), ensuring scalability, reliability, and performance.

 - Build and maintain infrastructure-as-code using Terraform, enabling repeatable and auditable deployments.

 - Develop and maintain CI/CD pipelines (e.g., CircleCI) to support rapid and reliable software delivery.

 - Implement and operate observability systems (metrics, logs, tracing) to monitor system health and performance.

 - Collaborate with backend and infrastructure teams to ensure services are designed for scalability, resilience, and operability.

 - Support integration with edge-deployed systems, including handling synchronization, connectivity, and deployment workflows across distributed environments.

 - Debug and resolve production issues across cloud infrastructure, networking, and application layers.

 - Contribute to best practices around security, reliability, and performance across the platform.



Qualifications

 - 5+ years of experience in DevOps, SRE, infrastructure or software engineering roles.

 - Strong experience with AWS (e.g., EC2, RDS, autoscaling, networking) in production environments.

 - Deep experience deploying and operating applications on Kubernetes.

 - Proficiency with Terraform or similar infrastructure-as-code tools.

 - Experience building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines (e.g., CircleCI or similar).

 - Strong scripting skills in Bash; proficiency in Python or Go is a plus.

 - Experience operating production systems with a focus on reliability, observability, and performance.

 - Strong understanding of networking

source_jobs.title: Technical Operations Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Rune

Rune Technologies builds mission-critical software for military logistics and sustainment. Our flagship product is TyrOS, an AI-enabled logistics command and control platform that gives commanders a single, real-time operating picture of their sustainment fight, from inventory and distribution to medical and operational planning, even in degraded and contested environments.



We're not a traditional defense contractor. We're a small, fast-moving team that combines deep operational experience in and with the DoD with the software discipline of the best product companies, and we apply that combination to a customer set that desperately needs it. We stay close to our users by embedding directly with military units during exercises and rotations, and we ship constantly based on what we learn.



The U.S. military and its allies face a real logistics modernization problem. We're building the software to solve it.





About the Role

As a Technical Operations Engineer, or TechOps Engineer, you will sit at the intersection of quality assurance (QA) testing, customer training, deployment support, and field operations. This role is designed for someone who understands military operations, cares deeply about the user, and wants to help bring modern technology to the soldiers, Marines, airmen, and operators still in the field.

You will support Rune’s Mission Success organization by helping test new software releases, prepare deployment packages, train users, collect product feedback, and assist with software and hardware deployments at customer sites. At headquarters, much of your work will focus on QA testing, training material development, and product readiness. In the field, you will help deploy Rune products, support software updates, train military users, and capture feedback that informs future product improvements.

This is a hands-on role for someone who wants to learn quickly, operate in high-stakes environments, and help ensure Rune’s products work reliably for the users who depend on them.

What You’ll Do

 - Validate new Rune software releases before they reach customers, ensuring workflows make sense for real military users.

 - Exercise the product like an operator would: planning missions, managing sustainment data, updating statuses, moving through workflows, and identifying where the software breaks down or becomes confusing.

 - Test end-to-end scenarios across TyrOS and other Rune products to confirm that the system is ready for exercises, demos, deployments, and customer use.

 - Identify bugs, workflow gaps, confusing product behavior, and training issues, then clearly communicate what happened and why it matters operationally.

 - Prepare deployment packages, including software, hardware, documentation, and training materials, for field use.

 - Provide hands-on technical support for Rune software and hardware products in forward-deployed environments.

 - Assist with installation, configuration, troubleshooting, and maintenance of deployed Rune systems; manage software updates in support of customer deployments.

 - Develop practical training materials that help new users understand and adopt Rune products quickly; train military users virtually, in classrooms, and in field environments.

 - Capture user feedback during training sessions, exercises, and deployments, and translate that feedback into clear product observations for the product and engineering teams.

 - Build and maintain deployment runbooks and a troubleshooting knowledge base, capturing field fixes so they are repeatable by others.

Qualifications

 - Experience serving in or working directly with the U.S. military.

 - Active U.S. Secret security clearance.

 - Basic understanding of computer hardware components such as CPUs, RAM, storage, cables, peripherals, and networking equipment.

 - Basic understanding of operating systems, especially Windows, Linux and command-line navigation.

 - Familiarity with fundamental networking conce

source_jobs.title: Backend Software Engineer - Maps & Routing
source_jobs.description:
About Rune

Rune Technologies builds mission-critical software for military logistics and sustainment. Our flagship product is TyrOS, an AI-enabled logistics command and control platform that gives commanders a single, real-time operating picture of their sustainment fight, from inventory and distribution to medical and operational planning, even in degraded and contested environments.



We're not a traditional defense contractor. We're a small, fast-moving team that combines deep operational experience in and with the DoD with the software discipline of the best product companies, and we apply that combination to a customer set that desperately needs it. We stay close to our users by embedding directly with military units during exercises and rotations, and we ship constantly based on what we learn.



The U.S. military and its allies face a real logistics modernization problem. We're building the software to solve it.



About the Role
At Rune Technologies, we are building the logistics technology backbone that the U.S. and allied militaries need to dominate in large-scale combat operations. As a Backend Software Engineer at Rune, you will be part of a high-impact team working at the intersection of advanced logistics algorithms, modern AI-enabled software, and complex system integrations, all in support of the warfighter at the tactical edge.

You'll join us in our mission to bring 21st-century software to the sustainment domain: modernizing inventory management, transportation optimization, and route planning with state-of-the-art user experiences. You'll work closely with a team of experienced engineers, product designers, and operational experts who care deeply about delivering real-world impact.

As our Backend Software Engineer for Maps & Routing, you will own the routing layer of TyrOS end to end. This means taking full responsibility for our ground routing infrastructure, built on Valhalla, and independently designing and implementing new capabilities on top of it. This is not a geospatial visualization role. We are looking for someone who has worked on routing engines themselves: the graph structures, cost functions, and edge-case logic that determine how a vehicle actually gets from A to B, especially when the environment is degraded, contested, or constantly changing.

What You'll Do

 - Own the design, development, and ongoing maintenance of Rune's routing layer, built on Valhalla, as the sole engineer responsible for this system.

 - Extend the existing routing foundation with new capabilities, including multi-modal transport routing, time-dependent cost modeling, and integration with operational data sources such as weather, terrain, and road availability.

 - Collaborate with the broader platform team to expose routing functionality through clean APIs and integrate it into TyrOS workflows.

 - Participate in technical design discussions and code reviews, contributing to a culture of engineering rigor and mission focus.

 - Triage and debug routing issues in production, ensuring reliability and rapid iteration in support of operational needs.

 - Work closely with engineers, product managers, and military stakeholders to ensure the routing layer meets real-world sustainment requirements at the tactical edge.



Qualifications

 - 4–6 years of experience in software engineering, with a demonstrated focus on routing, navigation, or transportation systems

 - Direct hands-on experience building or extending a routing engine (Valhalla, OSRM, Graphhopper, or equivalent)

 - Proficiency in C++ or Rust as a primary language; familiarity with Go is a plus.

 - Comfort owning a technical layer - you can design, implement, debug, and iterate independently.

 - Strong sense of ownership and a bias for action.



Desired Qualifications

 - Direct experience with Valhalla

 - Experience with multi-modal transport routing (ground, air, maritime) beyond single-mode use cases.

 - Prior work at a logistics, mapping, or supply

source_jobs.title: Head of Mission Success
source_jobs.description:
About Rune

Rune Technologies builds mission-critical software for military logistics and sustainment. Our flagship product is TyrOS, an AI-enabled logistics command and control platform that gives commanders a single, real-time operating picture of their sustainment fight, from inventory and distribution to medical and operational planning, even in degraded and contested environments.



We're not a traditional defense contractor. We're a small, fast-moving team that combines deep operational experience in and with the DoD with the software discipline of the best product companies, and we apply that combination to a customer set that desperately needs it. We stay close to our users by embedding directly with military units during exercises and rotations, and we ship constantly based on what we learn.



The U.S. military and its allies face a real logistics modernization problem. We're building the software to solve it.





About the Role

As Head of Mission Success, you will lead the deployment of Rune products and lead all aspects of programmatic execution for the company. You will be responsible for programmatic timelines, synchronization across efforts, staffing, and coordination across cloud and edge environments with customers from the Joint Force and allied nations.

You must have a technical aptitude as well as a deep understanding of military sustainment, coupled with demonstrated leadership experience of teams in high-stress and high-stakes environments. This is a customer-facing role where you will be engaging with senior military leaders to ensure alignment and buy-in. This is a role with deep cross-functional expectations, coordinating and working closely with the company's Head of Software and Head of Growth ensuring Mission Success, Growth, Engineering, and Product are synchronized across all deployment and execution efforts. Rune's Head of Mission Success reports directly to the company's CEO and is an integral part of the leadership team as Rune scales.

You will lead and grow a team of dedicated Mission Managers leading active efforts across the Joint Force, while remaining deeply hands-on in implementation and technical execution. You will define Rune's programmatic direction, set standards for performance and execution, and lead deployment of teams and systems globally.



What You’ll Do

 - Work side-by-side with our customers and senior military leaders to understand their unique challenges and represent the customer through your Mission Success team to continue shaping product development and direction.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with Rune Engineering, Product, Growth and Executive teams to ensure each team is engaged and working towards customer success.

 - Bring a deep understanding of military logistics operations to the Rune team, using your background and skillset to further expand and refine Rune product offerings.

 - Be comfortable leading a growing team of dedicated Mission Managers, providing clear guidance, direction, expectations and mentorship to the team as it scales.

 - Be a do-er who wants to understand customer problems and highly technical solutions to those problems and is not afraid to dive in personally to solve problems and ensure success.

 - Lead all programmatic deployment of Rune technology and products for testing, evaluation and operational use at government facilities and military bases.



Qualifications

 - Minimum of 10 years of experience serving in the US military; service in logistics or sustainment leadership roles preferred.

 - Possess an understanding of software development, and the unique deployment and accreditation aspects of building and operationalizing software within the military.

 - Excellent organizational, leadership, and decision-making skills.

 - Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills to interact with customers, team members, and senior leadership.

 - Ability to lead a cross-disciplinary team, and interface with other teams ac

Highly competitive equity grants are also  included in the majority of full time offers and are considered part of Rune's total compensation package. Benefits include:

 - Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans; premiums 100% covered by Rune for all employees; exceptionally low premiums for spouses and dependents

 - Basic life insurance and disability 100% covered for all employees by Rune; option to purchase additional life insurance available

 - ‘Take the time off that you need, when you need it’ paid time off, not accrual based

 - Generous company holiday calendar including a holiday shutdown in Decembe

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Rune

Rune Technologies builds mission-critical software for military logistics and sustainment. Our flagship product is TyrOS, an AI-enabled logistics command and control platform that gives commanders a single, real-time operating picture of their sustainment fight, from inventory and distribution to medical and operational planning, even in degraded and contested environments.



We're not a traditional defense contractor. We're a small, fast-moving team that combines deep operational experience in and with the DoD with the software discipline of the best product companies, and we apply that combination to a customer set that desperately needs it. We stay close to our users by embedding directly with military units during exercises and rotations, and we ship constantly based on what we learn.



The U.S. military and its allies face a real logistics modernization problem. We're building the software to solve it.





About the Role

We are seeking Forward Deployed Software Engineers to join our expanding team in the DC area. In this role, you’ll take on a diverse set of challenges spanning full-stack development, networking, systems integration, and user-focused problem solving—always balancing speed, scalability, and sound engineering judgment. You’ll collaborate closely with Product Engineering and Design, while working hand-in-hand with our Mission Success team to deliver end-to-end solutions that empower military logisticians. Our engineers thrive on ownership and accountability, approaching every project with a practical, execution-oriented mindset. You’ll work in small teams with minimal supervision and own end-to-end execution of high-stakes projects for our military customers. The right candidate will consistently deliver reliable, timely, and high-quality outcomes while keeping our mission at the forefront of decision-making.



What You’ll Do

 - Work side-by-side with end users and stakeholders to understand their toughest challenges, then design and deliver software solutions that directly address those needs.

 - Take full ownership of features from concept to deployment, including prototyping, integrating with existing systems, and ensuring performance in demanding real-world environments.

 - Collaborate across engineering, product, design, and operations teams to build scalable systems, prioritize work, and drive complex projects to completion.

 - Continuously monitor and improve system reliability and performance by instrumenting code, analyzing metrics, diagnosing issues, and iterating on fixes.

 - Balance short-term urgency with long-term maintainability—making thoughtful trade-offs that keep solutions practical, effective, and mission-focused.

 - Travel roughly ~25% to deploy, field-test, and support software systems, gathering feedback directly from users to ensure success in operational settings.
   
   

Qualifications

 - Strong coding skills with demonstrated proficiency in programming languages such as Go, C++, TypeScript/JavaScript, or similar.

 - High ownership mindset; we’re looking for a motivated individual who is biased to action and to assume responsibility for all aspects of the development and deployment of our software for a specific military customer.

 - Demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively in teams of technical and non-technical individuals, and comfortable working in a rapidly changing environment with dynamic objectives and iteration with users.

 - Strong communication abilities, with an interest and aptitude to lead customer-facing engineering interactions.

 - Active U.S. secret security clearance required; top secret preferred.
   
   

Desired Qualifications

 - Experience working on software in defense, aerospace, or other complex operational domains.

 - Familiarity with logistics, supply chain, or sustainment challenges — especially in military contexts.

 - Experience deploying software to production environments and managing cloud infrastructure (e.g. A

source_jobs.title: Backend Software Engineer - Generalist
source_jobs.description:
About Rune

Rune Technologies builds mission-critical software for military logistics and sustainment. Our flagship product is TyrOS, an AI-enabled logistics command and control platform that gives commanders a single, real-time operating picture of their sustainment fight, from inventory and distribution to medical and operational planning, even in degraded and contested environments.



We're not a traditional defense contractor. We're a small, fast-moving team that combines deep operational experience in and with the DoD with the software discipline of the best product companies, and we apply that combination to a customer set that desperately needs it. We stay close to our users by embedding directly with military units during exercises and rotations, and we ship constantly based on what we learn.



The U.S. military and its allies face a real logistics modernization problem. We're building the software to solve it.





About the Role

At Rune Technologies, we are building the logistics technology backbone that the U.S. and allied militaries need to dominate in large-scale combat operations. As a Backend Software Engineer at Rune, you will be part of a high-impact team working at the intersection of advanced logistics algorithms, modern AI enabled software, and complex system integrations — all in support of the warfighter at the tactical edge.

You’ll join us in our mission to bring 21st-century software to the sustainment domain — modernizing inventory management, transportation optimization, and predictive maintenance with state-of-the-art user experiences. You’ll work closely with a team of experienced engineers, product designers, and operational experts who care deeply about delivering real-world impact.



What You’ll Do

 - Design, build, and deploy robust software solutions that address the DoD’s hardest logistics technology challenges.

 - Collaborate with the team to develop TyrOS, our platform for modernizing tactical and operational logistics.

 - Build and iterate on applications that deliver modern, intuitive experiences to users at the tactical edge.

 - Integrate logistics data from diverse systems to enable real-time visibility, predictive insights, and optimized workflows.

 - Participate in technical design discussions and code reviews, contributing to a culture of excellence, collaboration, and mission focus.

 - Triage and debug issues in production, ensuring high reliability and rapid iteration in support of operational needs.

 - Work closely with military stakeholders, engineers, and product managers to ensure our technology meets real-world sustainment requirements.
   
   

Qualifications

 - 4+ years of experience in software engineering, preferably building and deploying production-grade web applications or distributed systems.

 - Strong coding skills with demonstrated proficiency in programming languages such as Go, C++, TypeScript/JavaScript, or similar.

 - Experience working with data pipelines, APIs, and system integrations.

 - Strong sense of ownership and a bias for action — you thrive in fast-paced, dynamic environments where your work directly impacts mission outcomes.

 - Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to translate user needs into technical solutions.
   
   

Desired Qualifications

 - Experience working on software in defense, aerospace, or other complex operational domains.

 - Familiarity with logistics, supply chain, or sustainment challenges — especially in military contexts.

 - Experience deploying software to production environments and managing cloud infrastructure (e.g. AWS, Kubernetes) as well as Edge infrastructure.

 - Exposure to data engineering, analytics, or machine learning in operational contexts.
   
   

Benefits

Rune offers top-tier benefits for full-time employees to include a full suite of insurance options at no cost for employees and low-cost to spouses and dependents. Highly competitive equity grants are also  includ

source_jobs.title: Backend Software Engineer - GenAI
source_jobs.description:
About Rune

Rune Technologies builds mission-critical software for military logistics and sustainment. Our flagship product is TyrOS, an AI-enabled logistics command and control platform that gives commanders a single, real-time operating picture of their sustainment fight, from inventory and distribution to medical and operational planning, even in degraded and contested environments.



We're not a traditional defense contractor. We're a small, fast-moving team that combines deep operational experience in and with the DoD with the software discipline of the best product companies, and we apply that combination to a customer set that desperately needs it. We stay close to our users by embedding directly with military units during exercises and rotations, and we ship constantly based on what we learn.



The U.S. military and its allies face a real logistics modernization problem. We're building the software to solve it.



About the Role
At Rune Technologies, we are building the logistics technology backbone that the U.S. and allied militaries need to dominate in large-scale combat operations. As a Backend Software Engineer at Rune, you will be part of a high-impact team working at the intersection of advanced logistics algorithms, modern AI enabled software, and complex system integrations -- all in support of the warfighter at the tactical edge.
You'll join us in our mission to bring 21st-century software to the sustainment domain -- modernizing inventory management, transportation optimization, and predictive maintenance with state-of-the-art user experiences. You'll work closely with a team of experienced engineers, product designers, and operational experts who care deeply about delivering real-world impact.


As a Backend Software Engineer focused on Saga -- Rune's AI agentic layer within TyrOS -- you will own the design and implementation of production GenAI systems that bring intelligent, autonomous capability to the sustainment domain. This is a backend engineering role first: we're looking for someone who has built real AI systems from the ground up, worked closely with engineers and operational experts, and cares about shipping software that actually works in the field.




What You'll Do

 - Design, build, and deploy production-grade GenAI systems -- including RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM integrations, and tool-call frameworks -- in support of Saga, Rune's AI agentic product layer.

 - Build and maintain evaluation and testing harnesses to validate LLM output quality and catch regressions in production.

 - Collaborate with the broader platform and product teams to integrate AI capabilities into TyrOS workflows.

 - Participate in technical design discussions and code reviews, contributing to a culture of engineering rigor and mission focus.

 - Triage and debug issues in production, ensuring reliability and rapid iteration in support of operational needs.

 - Work closely with engineers, product managers, and military stakeholders to ensure AI systems meet real-world sustainment requirements.



Qualifications

 - 4+ years of experience in software engineering, with a strong backend foundation -- distributed systems, APIs, production-grade software.

 - Hands-on experience building GenAI systems: vector databases, LLM integration, RAG architectures, and evaluation harnesses for LLM output.

 - Familiarity with tool routing, tool-call validation, and common LLM failure modes.

 - Understanding of the science underlying the systems you build -- not just how to wire them together, but why they work and where they break.

 - Background at an AI-native company, or a clear transition from backend engineering into AI specialization.

 - Strong sense of ownership and a bias for action.



Desired Qualifications

 - Experience with fine-tuning or model evaluation workflows.

 - Familiarity with AI infrastructure: embedding pipelines and inference optimization.

 - Experience working on software in defense, aer

source_jobs.title: Lead Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
About Rune

Rune Technologies builds mission-critical software for military logistics and sustainment. Our flagship product is TyrOS, an AI-enabled logistics command and control platform that gives commanders a single, real-time operating picture of their sustainment fight, from inventory and distribution to medical and operational planning, even in degraded and contested environments.



We're not a traditional defense contractor. We're a small, fast-moving team that combines deep operational experience in and with the DoD with the software discipline of the best product companies, and we apply that combination to a customer set that desperately needs it. We stay close to our users by embedding directly with military units during exercises and rotations, and we ship constantly based on what we learn.



The U.S. military and its allies face a real logistics modernization problem. We're building the software to solve it.



About the Role

A soldier routing a resupply convoy through contested terrain needs to make the right call in seconds - often with incomplete information, no connectivity, and no formal logistics training. That's the design problem at Rune.

We believe software quality is a strategic advantage. The difference between a clear interface and a confusing one can determine whether a mission succeeds. Design isn't a downstream function here - it's central to the bet we're making on TyrOS.

We're looking for a lead designer with high agency, great taste, and the craft to back it up. As our Lead Product Designer, you'll have deep ownership over the TyrOS product experience - across web and edge-deployed systems, building solutions around maps, real-time data, forecasting, mission planning, and convoy routing. You'll have more direct access to real users in real conditions than almost anywhere else you could work.



What you'll do

 - Own end-to-end design for critical TyrOS workflows, from problem framing and discovery through prototypes, shipped UI, and iteration

 - Work directly with logisticians, planners, and operators through field feedback, embedded research, and direct collaboration to understand their mental models, constraints, and environments

 - Design interfaces, user journeys, and interaction models for complex, stateful workflows across planning, monitoring, and decision-support scenarios

 - Shape and evolve our design system: patterns, components, interaction principles, and visual language

 - Collaborate tightly with product and engineering to scope iterations and ensure high-fidelity implementation

 - Bring a strong point of view to roadmap discussions - advocate for user needs, clarity, and simplicity in high-stakes contexts



Qualifications

 - 6-10+ years designing complex digital products, with a portfolio showing real shipped work, clear problem framing, and strong visual craft

 - Deep interaction design and IA skills - you can untangle dense, stateful workflows and make them feel obvious

 - Strong visual and systems design instincts: typography, spacing, hierarchy, and motion that feel cohesive

 - Experience as a true cross-functional partner to PM and engineering, not a downstream service

 - Proficiency in Figma and comfort moving between rapid exploration and high-fidelity, polished work

 - Clear communicator across audiences - designers, engineers, and operational stakeholders alike



Desired Qualifications

 - Experience with AI-enabled prototyping

 - Experience designing tools for logistics, operations, defense, healthcare, or other complex operational domains

 - Familiarity with geospatial or time-based data visualization (maps, timelines, charts)

 - Experience working in fast-paced environments with evolving requirements and mission-driven goals
   
   

Benefits

Rune offers top-tier benefits for full-time employees to include a full suite of insurance options at no cost for employees and low-cost to spouses and dependents. Highly competitive equity grants are also  in

source_jobs.title: Front End Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Rune

Rune Technologies builds mission-critical software for military logistics and sustainment. Our flagship product is TyrOS, an AI-enabled logistics command and control platform that gives commanders a single, real-time operating picture of their sustainment fight, from inventory and distribution to medical and operational planning, even in degraded and contested environments.



We're not a traditional defense contractor. We're a small, fast-moving team that combines deep operational experience in and with the DoD with the software discipline of the best product companies, and we apply that combination to a customer set that desperately needs it. We stay close to our users by embedding directly with military units during exercises and rotations, and we ship constantly based on what we learn.



The U.S. military and its allies face a real logistics modernization problem. We're building the software to solve it.



About the Role

At Rune Technologies, we are building the logistics technology backbone that the U.S. and allied militaries need to dominate in large-scale combat operations. As a Front End Software Engineer at Rune, you will be part of a high-impact team working at the intersection of modern web engineering, operational logistics software, and mission-critical user experiences -- all in support of the warfighter at the tactical edge.

You'll join us in our mission to bring 21st-century software to the sustainment domain -- modernizing inventory management, transportation optimization, and predictive maintenance through intuitive, high-performance applications. You'll work closely with product designers, backend engineers, and operational experts to build software that transforms complex logistics workflows into powerful and accessible user experiences.

This is a front-end engineering role first and foremost. We're looking for someone who has dedicated their career to building sophisticated web applications, possesses deep expertise in React or Angular, TypeScript, and modern JavaScript, and takes pride in owning front-end architecture and technical direction. The ideal candidate is a builder who has created significant products from the ground up, thrives in fast-moving environments, and cares deeply about delivering software that performs reliably in real-world operational settings.



What You'll Do

 - Design, build, and maintain complex, production-grade front-end applications using React (preferred) or Angular, TypeScript, and modern JavaScript.

 - Own front-end architecture decisions, establishing scalable patterns, frameworks, and engineering best practices that support long-term product growth.

 - Develop intuitive user experiences that enable operators to rapidly understand and act on complex logistics and operational data.

 - Collaborate closely with product designers, backend engineers, and military subject matter experts to translate mission requirements into high-quality software.

 - Build data-intensive interfaces, visualizations, and real-time user experiences that surface critical operational insights.

 - Integrate with APIs, WebSocket-based services, and distributed backend systems to deliver responsive, high-performance applications.

 - Contribute to code reviews, technical design discussions, and engineering standards that raise the quality and maintainability of the platform.

 - Diagnose and resolve performance bottlenecks, usability issues, and production incidents to ensure reliability in demanding operational environments.

 - Help shape the future of Rune's front-end platform by identifying opportunities for technical innovation, improved developer experience, and enhanced user impact.



Qualifications

 - Expert in React (preferred) or Angular — one must be primary, not both at a surface level

 - TypeScript and JavaScript as primary languages; strong command of HTML

 - 4+ years of experience with 75%+ in pure front end roles

 - Has built significant, non-trivial front-e

source_jobs.title: Backend Software Engineer - Simulations
source_jobs.description:
About Rune

Rune Technologies builds mission-critical software for military logistics and sustainment. Our flagship product is TyrOS, an AI-enabled logistics command and control platform that gives commanders a single, real-time operating picture of their sustainment fight, from inventory and distribution to medical and operational planning, even in degraded and contested environments.



We're not a traditional defense contractor. We're a small, fast-moving team that combines deep operational experience in and with the DoD with the software discipline of the best product companies, and we apply that combination to a customer set that desperately needs it. We stay close to our users by embedding directly with military units during exercises and rotations, and we ship constantly based on what we learn.



The U.S. military and its allies face a real logistics modernization problem. We're building the software to solve it.



About the Role
At Rune Technologies, we are building the logistics technology backbone that the U.S. and allied militaries need to dominate in large-scale combat operations. As a Backend Software Engineer at Rune, you will be part of a high-impact team working at the intersection of advanced logistics algorithms, modern AI-enabled software, and complex system integrations — all in support of the warfighter at the tactical edge.

You'll join us in our mission to bring 21st-century software to the sustainment domain: modernizing inventory management, transportation optimization, and predictive planning with state-of-the-art user experiences. You'll work closely with a team of experienced engineers, product designers, and operational experts who care deeply about delivering real-world impact.

As our Backend Software Engineer for Simulations, you will own the simulations workstream within TyrOS, working closely with our forecaster lead and the broader platform team. The primary focus is Rune's forecasting system, building the production software that models, predicts, and reasons about logistics outcomes in complex operational environments. This role calls for a strong software engineer who is also mathematically and scientifically fluent: someone who can work in formulas, reason about physical systems, and build production-quality software around that reasoning.



What You'll Do

 - Build and own production-grade simulation and forecasting software, with a primary focus on Rune's logistics forecaster.

 - Work with mathematical models, numerical methods, and scientific formulas to implement software where correctness is defined by the underlying physics or math, not just user acceptance.

 - Collaborate closely with the forecaster lead and platform team to integrate simulation capabilities into TyrOS workflows.

 - Participate in technical design discussions and code reviews, contributing to a culture of engineering rigor and mission focus.

 - Triage and debug issues in production, ensuring reliability and rapid iteration in support of operational needs.

 - Work closely with engineers, product managers, and military stakeholders to ensure simulation and forecasting systems meet real-world sustainment requirements.



Qualifications

 - 4+ years of experience in software engineering, with demonstrated experience building software in a scientific or mathematically complex domain: sensor algorithms, motion planning, simulation, forecasting, or similar.

 - Ability to work with mathematical models and numerical methods in code; comfort reasoning about correctness in terms of physics or math, not just software behavior.

 - Proficiency in Python, C++, or Go.

 - Strong software engineering foundation - this role requires production-quality engineering, not analysis or research.

 - Math or science background, through degree, graduate work, or professional domain experience.

 - Strong sense of ownership and a bias for action.



Desired Qualifications

 - Experience at a defense-adjacent autonomous systems comp

notes: Defense contractor with AI-enabled logistics platform
